2605.10806.title: PhyGround: Benchmarking Physical Reasoning in Generative World Models
2605.10806.paper_summary: Generative world models are increasingly used for video generation, where learned simulators are expected to capture the physical rules that govern real-world dynamics. However, evaluating whether generated videos actually follow these rules remains challenging. Existing physics-focused video benchmarks have made important progress, but they still face three key challenges, including the coarse evaluation frameworks that hide law-specific failures, response biases and fatigue that undermine the validity of annotation judgments, and automated evaluators that are insufficiently physics-aware or difficult to audit. To address those challenges, we introduce PhyGround, a criteria-grounded benchmark for evaluating physical reasoning in video generation. The benchmark contains 250 curated prompts, each augmented with an expected physical outcome, and a taxonomy of 13 physical laws across solid-body mechanics, fluid dynamics, and optics. Each law is operationalized through observable sub-questions to enable per-law diagnostics. We evaluate eight modern video generation models through a large-scale, quality-controlled human study, grounded on social science lab experiment design. A total of 459 annotators provided 5,796 complete annotations and over 37.4K fine-grained labels; after quality control, the retained annotations exhibited high split-half model-ranking correlations (Spearman's rho > 0.90). To support reproducible automated evaluation, we release PhyJudge-9B, an open physics-specialized VLM judge. PhyJudge-9B achieves substantially lower aggregate relative bias than Gemini-3.1-Pro (3.3% vs. 16.6%). We release prompts, human annotations, model checkpoints, and evaluation code on the project page https://phyground.github.io/.
2605.10806.paper_summary_zh: 生成世界模型在視頻生成中越來越常被使用，學習到的模擬器預期能捕捉支配現實世界動態的物理規則。<lb/>然而，評估生成的視頻是否實際遵循這些規則仍然具有挑戰性。<lb/>現有的以物理為重點的視頻基準已取得重要進展，但仍面臨三個關鍵挑戰，包括隱藏特定法律失敗的粗略評估框架、削弱標註判斷有效性的反應偏見和疲勞，以及不足以理解物理或難以審計的自動評估器。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們介紹了PhyGround，一個基於標準的基準，用於評估視頻生成中的物理推理。<lb/>該基準包含250個精心策劃的提示，每個提示都附有預期的物理結果，以及涵蓋固體力學、流體力學和光學的13條物理法則的分類法。<lb/>每條法則通過可觀察的子問題進行操作化，以便進行逐法則診斷。<lb/>我們通過一項大規模的、質量控制的人類研究評估了八個現代視頻生成模型，該研究基於社會科學實驗室實驗設計。<lb/>總共有459名標註者提供了5,796個完整的標註和超過37,400個細緻的標籤；經過質量控制後，保留的標註顯示出高的分半模型排名相關性（Spearman's rho > 0.90）。<lb/>為了支持可重複的自動評估，我們發布了PhyJudge-9B，一個開放的物理專用VLM評判器。<lb/>PhyJudge-9B的總體相對偏差顯著低於Gemini-3.1-Pro（3.3%對16.6%）。<lb/>我們在項目頁面https://phyground.github.io/上發布了提示、人類標註、模型檢查點和評估代碼。
2605.10806.author: Juyi Lin et.al.
2605.10806.authors: Juyi Lin, Arash Akbari, Yumei He, Lin Zhao, Haichao Zhang, Arman Akbari, Xingchen Xu, Zoe Y. Lu, Enfu Nan, Hokin Deng, Edmund Yeh, Sarah Ostadabbas, Yun Fu, Jennifer Dy, Pu Zhao, Yanzhi Wang
